title: v0.4.0 - MCP Protocol
description: Extensible tools: MCP client/server, dynamic tool discovery, config scopes, /mcp command

title: Add context window management and warnings
description:
Models with smaller context windows need aggressive context management.

## Approach: Aggressive Compaction
User prefers smaller/faster models with smart context management over large context models.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Token counting** - Track tokens per message, estimate total vs model limit
2. **Early warnings** - Alert at 50%, 70%, 90% capacity
3. **Aggressive summarization** - Summarize older messages when approaching limit
4. **Checkpointing** - Save conversation state for recovery
5. **Tool reminder injection** - Re-inject key tools in system prompt when context shrinks

## Model Context Sizes
- ministral-3:3b - 32k (fast, good for testing)
- gemma3:27b - 8k (needs aggressive compaction)
- llama3:8b - 8k
- mistral:7b - 32k

## Trade-offs
Smaller context + compaction = faster responses, lower cost
Larger context = simpler code, but slower/expensive

User preference: 'faster is better than better'

title: Reference: Guile 3.0.11 upstream study - native HTTP client available
description:
## Summary

Cloned Guile upstream from https://codeberg.org/guile/guile to ~/src/guile-upstream
to understand the Guile Scheme implementation and identify features for guile-sage.

## Key Findings

### Native HTTP Client (web/client.scm)

Guile has a built-in HTTP client in `(web client)`:

```scheme
(use-modules (web client))

;; Available procedures:
;; - http-get, http-post, http-put, http-delete, http-head, http-options, http-trace
;; - open-socket-for-uri
;; - current-http-proxy, current-https-proxy
;; - x509-certificate-directory
```

Features:
- Full HTTP/1.1 support
- HTTPS via GnuTLS autoload
- Certificate validation
- Proxy support (http_proxy, https_proxy env vars)
- String/bytevector bodies
- Keep-alive connections
- Streaming responses

**Consideration**: Could replace our curl-based HTTP in util.scm for v0.3+.

### Textual Ports (ice-9/textual-ports.scm)

We use `get-string-all` which is simply:
```scheme
(define (get-string-all port)
  (read-string port))
```

### Available SRFIs

Full list in module/srfi/. Notable ones we use:
- srfi-1: List library
- srfi-9: Records
- srfi-19: Time/date
- srfi-43: Vectors
- srfi-64: Testing

### Version Info

Current upstream: Guile 3.0.11

## Action Items

1. Consider native HTTP client for v0.3 (remove curl dependency)
2. Add SRFI-64 test framework usage
3. Use (web uri) for URL parsing instead of string manipulation

title: Replace custom JSON with guile-json library
description:
Replace hand-rolled JSON parser/writer in src/sage/util.scm with guile-json (https://github.com/aconchillo/guile-json).

## Current State
Custom implementation in util.scm:
- json-read-string - custom recursive descent parser
- json-write / json-write-string - custom serializer

## Target
Use guile-json:
```scheme
(use-modules (json))
(json-string->scm str)  ; parse
(scm->json-string obj)  ; serialize
```

## Benefits
- Battle-tested library
- Better error handling
- Maintained upstream
- Smaller codebase

## Library
https://github.com/aconchillo/guile-json
License: LGPL 3+

title: Implement intelligent context compaction with evals
description: Improve /compact to shrink context based on conversation intent. Create evals using larger model to measure compaction quality. Document and run experiments.

title: Auto-compaction at 95% token limit
description: Automatically compact context when approaching token limit (configurable, default 80%). Show status message. SAGE_COMPACTION_THRESHOLD env var.

title: Add log introspection tools for self-debugging
description:
Sage should be able to read and analyze its own logs for self-debugging and introspection.

## Current State
Tools ALREADY EXIST in tools.scm:
- `read_logs` - Read recent log entries (in *safe-tools*)
- `search_logs` - Search logs for pattern (in *safe-tools*)

The /logs REPL command also uses read-recent-logs.

## Gap Analysis
The tools exist but models aren't discovering/using them. Issues:
1. **Discoverability** - Models don't know about these tools (need better /tools output or documentation)
2. **Session history** - No tool to see tool call history for current session
3. **Statistics** - No tool to get tool execution stats (success rate, timing)
4. **Context** - The model asking about introspection suggests it doesn't know these tools exist

## Suggested Enhancements
1. Ensure read_logs/search_logs appear in tool descriptions sent to LLM
2. Add session_history tool - returns this session's tool calls
3. Add tool_stats tool - returns execution statistics
4. Better /tools output showing tool categories (introspection, file ops, etc.)

## Related
Models should be prompted to use these tools when debugging failures.

title: Fix 'used before definition' warnings in sage/repl.scm
description: The *commands* alist at lines 99-125 references cmd-* functions using unquote, but these functions are defined later in the file (starting line 129). This causes Guile 'used before definition' warnings during compilation and a segfault at runtime. Fix: move *commands* definition to after all cmd-* function definitions.
close_reason: Fixed by moving *commands* definition after cmd-* function definitions. Added tests/test-repl.scm with 13 passing tests.

title: GitHub account for guile-sage agent identity
description:
## Summary

Created GitHub account for guile-sage autonomous agent commits.

## Account Details

- **Username**: guile-sage (pending confirmation)
- **Email**: jasonwalsh+guile-sage@gmail.com
- **Commit email**: guile-sage@users.noreply.github.com (after setup)

## Setup Steps

1. [x] Create GitHub account
2. [ ] Enable "Keep my email addresses private" in Settings → Emails
3. [ ] Update scripts/sage-commit.sh with noreply email
4. [ ] Update CONTRIBUTING.org with final email
5. [ ] Optional: Add GPG key for verified commits

## Credential Storage

Use `pass` for password management:
```bash
pass insert github.com/guile-sage
```

Do NOT store credentials in .env or repo files.

## Related

- scripts/sage-commit.sh - commit helper script
- CONTRIBUTING.org - documents identity conventions

title: Replace shell curl with guile-curl library
description:
Replace shell-based curl calls in util.scm with guile-curl library for proper Guile semantics.

## Current State
- `http-get-curl` and `http-post-curl` in src/sage/util.scm use `(system "curl ...")`
- Requires temp files for input/output
- Shell escaping needed for safety
- Error handling via parsing exit codes

## Target State
Use `(use-modules (curl))` with:
- `curl-easy-init` / `curl-easy-cleanup` for handle management
- `curl-easy-setopt` for URL, headers, POST data
- `curl-easy-perform` returns body directly (no temp files)
- `curl-error-code` for proper error handling

## Benefits
- No temp file management
- No shell escaping needed
- Native Guile error handling
- Cleaner, more maintainable code
- Same libcurl backend (HTTPS works)

## Blockers
- guile-curl not in FreeBSD pkg/ports
- Need to build from source: https://github.com/spk121/guile-curl

## Files to Modify
- src/sage/util.scm: Replace http-get-curl, http-post-curl
- Remove shell-escape helper if no longer needed

## Example API
```scheme
(use-modules (curl))
(let ((h (curl-easy-init)))
  (curl-easy-setopt h 'url "https://api.example.com")
  (curl-easy-setopt h 'post #t)
  (curl-easy-setopt h 'postfields (string->utf8 body))
  (let ((response (utf8->string (curl-easy-perform h #t))))
    (curl-easy-cleanup h)
    response))
```

title: Add optional IRC-based agent message queue (guile-irc)
description:
Use IRC as a message queue for agent communication via guile-irc library.

## Library
- **Source**: [rekado/guile-irc](https://github.com/rekado/guile-irc) or [fbs/guile-irc](https://github.com/fbs/guile-irc)
- **Install**: `guix install guile-irc` (available in Guix)
- **License**: LGPL 2.1+
- **Docs**: http://fbs.github.io/guile-irc/

## Basic Usage
```scheme
(use-modules (irc irc) (irc handlers))
(define irc (make-irc #:nick "sage-agent" #:server "host.lan" #:port 6667))
(do-connect irc)
(do-join irc "#sage-agents")
(do-privmsg irc "#sage-agents" "Task completed: build_call_graph")
```

## Channels (SageNet on host.lan:6667)
- #sage-agents - Agent coordination
- #sage-tasks - Task updates
- #sage-debug - Debug/monitoring

## Implementation Plan
1. Add (sage irc) module wrapping guile-irc
2. Register tools: irc_send, irc_join, irc_read
3. Optional: Auto-join on startup if IRC configured
4. Log tool calls to #sage-debug

## Model Config
Keep gemma3:27b as fallback, use ministral-3:3b for speed testing.

title: Add keybase chat notification for sage-to-user communication
description:
Enable guile-sage to send notifications to user via keybase chat.

## Use Case
When sage completes long tasks, encounters errors, or needs user attention, it can send a keybase message.

## Implementation
```scheme
(define (notify-user msg)
  (system (format #f "keybase chat send jwalsh '~a'" (shell-escape msg))))
```

## Examples
- Task complete: 'Finished refactoring session.scm - 5 files changed'
- Error: 'Build failed in tools.scm:234 - missing dependency'
- Question: 'Need clarification on API design - check IRC #sage-agents'

## Prerequisites
- keybase running and logged in
- User's keybase username configured

title: Replace shell calls with native Guile/gash-utils
description:
Audit shell calls in codebase and replace where possible with native Guile or gash-utils.

## Shell Call Audit

### CAN Replace (mkdir -p calls):
- src/sage/logging.scm:86 - mkdir -p for log dir
- src/sage/session.scm:44 - mkdir -p for session dir  
- src/sage/config.scm:196 - mkdir -p for config dir

**Solution**: Use native `(mkdir dir)` with recursive helper or `(ice-9 ftw)`.

### CAN Replace (which command):
- src/sage/agent.scm:68 - `which bd` to check if bd exists

**Solution**: Use `(search-path (parse-path (getenv "PATH")) "bd")` from core Guile.

### CANNOT Replace (by design):
- src/sage/tools.scm:237,256,274,300,323,375,400,418,444 - Tool execution (shell commands are the feature)
- src/sage/agent.scm:79,96,112,133 - bd CLI integration (external tool)
- src/sage/main.scm:68 - check-config.sh script call

### COULD Replace (curl usage):
- src/sage/util.scm:240,279 - curl for HTTPS requests

**Notes**: Native (web client) exists but has gnutls cert issues with HTTPS noted in code. Consider guile-gnutls or keeping curl fallback.

## Potential Library Additions

1. **gash-utils** - POSIX utilities in pure Scheme (mkdir, cat, etc.)
2. **guile-gcrypt** - Crypto if needed (hashing, etc.)
3. **guile-json** - Already have custom JSON, but official lib may be more robust

## Priority
Low - current shell calls work, but pure Scheme would improve portability and reduce external dependencies.
